minimal_context: The student is working individually in the computer lab, watching an English-language instructional video with Spanish subtitles about writing out math equations.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please watch the video on your computer screen to see how to write out the math equations.

STUDENT: I am trying to watch it, but the video is in English and I have to use the Spanish subtitles.

TUTOR: Just keep watching and try your best to follow along.

STUDENT: It is too fast. It requires me to read subtitles while also watching someone write out math equations, and the subtitles have unfamiliar terms and new words.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: (Visibly asking the teacher for help) I need you to show me how to do it. The teacher can teach me more than the video.

options.A: Check the student's progress on the platform every Sunday night to see what mistakes they are making and what math topics they are struggling with.
options.B: Set a timer to sound every minute, prompting the student to record whether they had positive interactions during the preceding interval by checking Yes or No at the computer.
options.C: Conduct careful pre-intervention functional assessments to identify relationships between the computer lab environment and the student's behavior while watching the videos.
options.D: Establish routines in the computer lab and make believe that the student is a part of that cycle while watching the instructional videos.
